name: Morocco
shortIntro: Moroccan football moves between street invention, sharp tactical detail and the roar of cities that treat match night as a shared ceremony.
afconHistory: Morocco won the African title in 1976 and has remained a constant AFCON reference through generations of technical teams and demanding supporters.
worldCupHistory: Morocco became the first African men's side to reach a World Cup semi-final in 2022 after earlier landmark campaigns including the 1986 round of 16.
iconicMoments: The 2022 run past Spain and Portugal

quiz.question: Which year brought Morocco its AFCON title?

Which stage did Morocco reach at the 1986 World Cup?
Which defender captained major Morocco eras?

name: Senegal
shortIntro: Senegalese football balances Dakar rhythm, fierce pressing and the calm confidence of a nation that keeps producing leaders for the biggest stages.
afconHistory: After near misses, Senegal lifted its first AFCON trophy in 2021, a title that crowned years of consistency and deep tournament squads.
worldCupHistory: Senegal announced itself globally by defeating defending champion France in 2002 and reaching the quarter-finals in its debut World Cup.

quiz.question: Senegal won its first AFCON title in which edition?

Which forward scored Senegal's winning AFCON final penalty in 2021?

name: Nigeria
shortIntro: Nigeria brings expressive attackers, unforgettable shirts and a national football conversation that never loses its appetite for flair.
afconHistory: Nigeria has won AFCON three times and has built one of the competition's deepest records of podium finishes and tournament drama.
worldCupHistory: The Super Eagles reached the World Cup round of 16 in 1994, 1998 and 2014, turning pace and confidence into a global identity.

quiz.question: What nickname belongs to Nigeria's men's national team?

name: Egypt
shortIntro: Egyptian football carries stadium theatre, technical craft and a history that stretches through the earliest chapters of the African game.
afconHistory: Egypt is AFCON's most decorated nation, with eras of dominance including the historic three-title run from 2006 to 2010.
worldCupHistory: Egypt returned to the World Cup in 2018 after earlier appearances in 1934 and 1990, each qualification carrying enormous national weight.

quiz.question: Which nation has the most AFCON titles?

name: Cameroon
shortIntro: Cameroon football is built on force, swagger and the belief that an African side can seize the world's attention at any moment.
afconHistory: Cameroon has claimed five AFCON titles, pairing powerful tournament teams with generations of famous forwards and goalkeepers.
worldCupHistory: The Indomitable Lions reached the 1990 World Cup quarter-finals, a breakthrough run led by unforgettable attacking moments.

quiz.question: Cameroon reached a World Cup quarter-final in which year?

name: Ivory Coast
shortIntro: Ivory Coast football blends street confidence, imposing athletes and a golden-generation memory that still shapes every orange wave.
afconHistory: The Elephants have won AFCON three times, with triumphs in 1992, 2015 and a dramatic home-title campaign in 2023.
worldCupHistory: Ivory Coast qualified for three consecutive World Cups from 2006 to 2014, arriving each time with heavyweight attacking talent.

name: Ghana
shortIntro: Ghana football carries academy craft, fierce tournament memory and the proud expectation attached to the Black Stars name.
afconHistory: Ghana won four AFCON titles and helped define the competition's early standards with disciplined and expressive teams.
worldCupHistory: Ghana reached the World Cup quarter-finals in 2010, coming one kick away from an African semi-final.

quiz.question: What nickname belongs to Ghana?

name: Algeria
shortIntro: Algerian football joins technical left-footed poetry, defiant tournament energy and supporters who turn each touch into emotion.
afconHistory: Algeria won AFCON in 1990 at home and again in 2019 with a composed, intense side that controlled a demanding tournament.
worldCupHistory: Algeria shocked West Germany in 1982 and pushed Germany to extra time in the 2014 round of 16.

name: Tunisia
shortIntro: Tunisia brings compact intelligence, intense derbies and a tournament temperament shaped by patience and collective structure.
afconHistory: Tunisia won AFCON in 2004 on home soil and has remained one of the continent's most frequent tournament qualifiers.
worldCupHistory: Tunisia earned Africa's first World Cup match win in 1978 and has kept returning to test organized football against elite sides.

name: South Africa
shortIntro: South African football mixes township technique, dance-like confidence and the huge emotional weight of football in a changing nation.
afconHistory: South Africa won AFCON in 1996 on home soil, a defining moment for Bafana Bafana and the country's sporting imagination.
worldCupHistory: South Africa appeared at the World Cup in 1998 and 2002 before hosting the 2010 tournament and scoring its opening goal.
